diagrams.title: B-Tree Node Structure
diagrams.description: Shows internal structure of a B-Tree node with 3 keys and 4 children
elements: Keys: k1, k2, k3 (where k1 < k2 < k3)
Child pointers: C0, C1, C2, C3
Key-Child relationship: C0 contains keys < k1, C1 contains keys between k1 and k2, C2 contains keys between k2 and k3, C3 contains keys > k3
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how keys divide the search space in B-Tree nodes

diagrams.title: Node Splitting in 2-4 Tree
diagrams.description: Shows the process of splitting a 4-node into two 2-nodes with middle key moving up
elements: Before split: 4-node with keys A,B,C and children P0,P1,P2,P3
After split: Two 2-nodes (A with P0,P1) and (C with P2,P3)
Middle key B moves up to parent node
Parent gains one key and one child
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the fundamental operation that maintains balance in 2-4/B-Trees

diagrams.title: B-Tree Height Comparison
diagrams.description: Comparison showing B-Tree with high branching factor vs binary tree
elements: B-Tree (m=5): Height 2 with many keys per node
Binary Tree: Height 4 with fewer keys
Shows why B-Trees have fewer disk accesses
Root at top, leaves at same bottom level
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why B-Trees minimize disk I/O operations

diagrams.title: 2-4 Tree Node Types
diagrams.description: Three types of nodes in a 2-4 tree with examples
elements: Shows sorted keys within each node type
diagrams.purpose: Learning the fundamental building blocks of 2-4 trees
